LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LENDLEASE CONSTRUCTION INC. FKA BOVIS LEND LEASE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT: THE COURT DENIES THIS MOTION IN ITS ENTIRETY. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANT NEGLIGENTLY RETAINED CONTROL OVER SAFETY CONDITIONS AT THE WORKSITE AND/OR DELEGATED ALL OF ITS SAFETY RESPONSIBILITIES TO THE SUBCONTRACTORS. RAMON NAZARIO (FOREMAN FOR SUBCONTRACTOR FOUR STAR ERECTORS) STATED THAT DEFENDANT WAS RESPONSIBLE FOR THE HOLE COVERINGS AND SHOULD HAVE COVERED THE HOLE THAT PLAINTIFF FELL THROUGH (PLAINTIFF'S'S ADDITIONAL FACTS 36, 45, 46 ). DEFENDANT'S SITE SPECIFIC SAFETY PERSON (LACEY HARSTON) TESTIFIED THAT DEFENDANT WAS ULTIMATELY ACCOUNTABLE FOR THE INCIDENT (PLAINTIFF'S ADDITIONAL FACT 162). ART. 10.1.1 OF THE CCSF/LEND LEASE CONTRACT PROVIDES THAT DEFENDANT 'SHALL BE SOLELY RESPONSIBLE FOR INITIATING, MAINTAINING, AND SUPERVISING ALL SAFETY PRECAUTIONS AND PROGRAMS IN CONNECTION WITH THE PERFORMANCE OF THE CONTRACT.' (SEE ALSO ART. 3.2.1). DEFENDANT'S PMK (JERRY MARCIS) AGREED THAT COVERING THE HOLES WAS DEFENDANT'S RESPONSIBILITY UNDER THE CCSF CONTRACT (PLAINTIFF'S ADDITIONAL FACT 166; MARCIS DEPO, 26:13-24) DEFENDANT'S SENIOR SUPERINTENDENT (MR. MCGUIRE) ADMITTED THAT IF NO ON-SITE COMPANY WAS RESPONSIBLE FOR THE GAP, IT WAS DEFENDANT'S JOB TO REMEDY THE SITUATION. HE EVEN STATES THAT DEFENDANT DID HAVE PROTECTIONS IN PLACE. (PLAINTIFF'S ADDITIONAL FACTS 143-144; MCGUIRE DEPO, 41:3-8, 42:16-22). LASTLY, THE DECK WEST CONTRACT PROVIDES THAT THE GENERAL CONTRACTOR WILL PROVIDE SAFETY PLANKING. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM AND CONTENT). JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LENDLEASE CONSTRUCTION INC. FKA BOVIS LEND LEASE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON INTERVENOR SEABRIGHT'S COMPLAINT: THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THERE IS NO CONFUSION REGARDING THE 'PRIME CONTRACT.' DEFENDANT IS CLEARLY REFERRING TO THE KWAN WO/LEND LEASE CONTRACT AS THE PRIME CONTRACT. FLORENCE KONG, THE PERSON THAT SIGNED THAT CONTRACT ON BEHALF OF KWAN WO, PROPERLY AUTHENTICATES THE CONTRACT. INTERVENOR FAILS TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER EX. C IS PART OF THAT CONTRACT. INTERVENOR FILED A COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION FOR SUBROGATION PURSUANT TO LABOR CODE 3850 ET SEQ. [SUBROGATION]. THE WAIVER OF SUBROGATION RIGHTS IN EX. C APPLIES TO INTERVENOR. INTERVENOR'S INSURED (DECK WEST) INCORPORATED THAT PROVISION INTO ITS CONTRACT WITH KWAN WO IN SECTION 1 OF THE DECK WEST/KWAN WO CONTRACT. INTERVENOR IS BOUND BY THAT WAIVER PURSUANT TO AM. CONT'L INS. CO. V. AM. CAS. CO. OF READING, PENN. (1999) 73 CAL.APP.4TH 508, 513 ('AN INSURER CANNOT ACQUIRE BY SUBROGATION ANYTHING TO WHICH THE INSURED HAS NO RIGHTS, AND MAY CLAIM NO RIGHTS WHICH THE INSURED DOES NOT HAVE.'). (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM AND CONTENT). JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

Off Calendar Oct-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JONATHAN DAVIS, ESQ., AND ZACHARIAH HANSEN, ESQ., 415.495.7800, FOR PLAINTIFF WINSLOW HEADRICK.
STEPHEN BARNES, ESQ., 650.991.5126, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT WILLIS CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC.
ANDREW KING, ESQ., 925.930.6600, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT FOUR STAR ERECTORS, INC.

REINA MINOYA, ESQ., AND IAN RAMBARRAN, ESQ., 916.444.7573, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT LENDLEASE CONSTRUCTION INC., FKA BOVIS LEND LEASE, INC.
KEITH COOPER, ESQ., 415.268.8000, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT RJS & ASSOCIATES, INC.
NADINE ADRIAN, ESQ., 310.579.2061, FOR INTERVENOR SEABRIGHT INSURANCE.

RULING: THE COURT ISSUES TENTATIVE RULING IN OPEN COURT. MATTER ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING REGARDING DEFENDANT LENDLEASE CONSTRUCTION INC. FKA BOVIS LEND LEASE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON INTERVENOR SEABRIGHT'S COMPLAINT:The Court grants the motion for summary judgment. There is no confusion regarding the 'prime contract.' Defendant is clearly referring to the Kwan Wo/Lend Lease contract as the prime contract. Florence Kong, the person that signed that contract on behalf of Kwan Wo, properly authenticates the contract. Intervenor fails to create a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Ex. C is part of that contract. Intervenor filed a complaint in intervention for subrogation pursuant to Labor Code 3850 et seq. [subrogation]. The waiver of subrogation rights in Ex. C applies to intervenor. Intervenor's insured (Deck West) incorporated that provision into its contract with Kwan Wo in section 1 of the Deck
West/Kwan Wo contract. Intervenor is bound by that waiver pursuant to Am. Cont'l Ins. Co. v. Am. Cas. Co. of Reading, Penn. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 508, 513 ('an insurer cannot acquire by subrogation anything to which the insured has no rights, and may claim no rights which the
insured does not have.'). (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order APPROVED AS TO FORM AND CONTENT). Judge: Leslie C. Nichols; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Cross Defendant Lend Lease (Us) Construction, Inc. Fka Bovis Lend'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JONATHAN DAVIS, ESQ., AND ZACHARIAH HANSEN, ESQ., 415.495.7800, FOR PLAINTIFF WINSLOW HEADRICK.
STEPHEN BARNES, ESQ., 650.991.5126, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT WILLIS CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC.
ANDREW KING, ESQ., 925.930.6600, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT FOUR STAR ERECTORS, INC.
DOUGLAS LIVINGSTON, ESQ., 925.934.6102, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT JOHN BERTOLDI INCORPORATED.
REINA MINOYA, ESQ., AND IAN RAMBARRAN, ESQ., 916.444.7573, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT LENDLEASE CONSTRUCTION INC., FKA BOVIS LEND LEASE, INC.
KEITH COOPER, ESQ., 415.268.8000, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT RJS & ASSOCIATES, INC.
NADINE ADRIAN, ESQ., 310.579.2061, FOR INTERVENOR SEABRIGHT INSURANCE.

RULING: THE COURT ISSUES TENTATIVE RULING IN OPEN COURT. MATTER ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING REGARDING DEFENDANT LENDLEASE CONSTRUCTION INC. FKA BOVIS LEND LEASE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT: The Court denies this motion in its entirety. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant negligently retained control over safety conditions at the worksite and/or delegated all of its safety responsibilities to the subcontractors. Ramon Nazario (foreman for subcontractor Four Star Erectors) stated that defendant was responsible for the hole coverings and should have covered the hole that plaintiff fell through (Plaintiff's's Additional Facts 36, 45, 46 ). Defendant's site specific safety person (Lacey Harston) testified that defendant was ultimately accountable for the incident (Plaintiff's Additional Fact 162). Art. 10.1.1 of the CCSF/Lend Lease contract provides that defendant 'shall be solely responsible for initiating, maintaining, and supervising all safety precautions and programs in connection with the performance of the Contract.' (See also Art. 3.2.1). Defendant's PMK (Jerry Marcis) agreed that covering the holes was defendant's responsibility under the CCSF contract (Plaintiff's Additional Fact 166; Marcis Depo, 26:13-24) Defendant's senior superintendent (Mr. McGuire) admitted that if no on-site company was responsible for the gap, it was defendant's job to remedy the situation. He even states that defendant did have protections in place. (Plaintiff's Additional Facts 143-144; McGuire Depo, 41:3-8, 42:16-22). Lastly, the Deck West contract provides that the general contractor will provide safety planking. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order APPROVED AS TO FORM AND CONTENT). Judge: Leslie C. Nichols; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

FKA BOVIS LEND LEASE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON ITS CROSS COMPLAINT AGAINST FOUR STAR ERECTORS, INC.: The motion is denied without prejudice. The Court determines that the trial judge should rule on the duty to defend issue. Weather Shield Mfg. Inc. (2008) 44 Cal.4th 541, 565, fn.12. Defendant is not prejudiced. It appears that defendant has a commercial general liability policy and is being defended.
